text.title: Asset Harvest Checklist Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that turn the harvest into wasted effort or worse. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Stale harvest: started > 30 days after sunset; indie cannot remember module load-bearing-ness; harvest skews to obvious assets, misses subtle ones.
failure-mode.repair: Don't bother with deep harvest; do a fast 2-hour skim of obvious S1 + S2 assets only. Document the skipped sections. Future sunsets MUST hit the 14-day window.

failure-mode.description: Client code accidentally harvested; surfaces months later when a different client recognises the code.
failure-mode.detector: extraction source path or git history reveals client engagement (client name in path, work-for-hire contract on the repo)
failure-mode.repair: Hard stop. Remove from starter kit. Scrub git history if the harvest commit is recent. Audit the rest of the starter kit for similar provenance issues. Document in personal IP register.

failure-mode.description: License drift: AGPL fragment ends up in MIT-declared starter kit, and the indie ships a closed-source bet on top of it.
failure-mode.detector: file-level license scan finds AGPL / SSPL / strong-copyleft headers in a MIT-declared starter kit
failure-mode.repair: Either re-license the starter kit OR remove the AGPL fragment OR isolate to a separate sub-folder with its own LICENSE. Run a license-compatibility check on every harvest.

failure-mode.description: Feature-specific code harvested under the illusion of generality; sits in starter-kit unused for 18 months.
failure-mode.detector: extractions[] generalisation = verbatim AND the asset has not been imported by any subsequent bet within 12 months
failure-mode.repair: Quarterly starter-kit audit: archive unused assets to an `archive/` folder. Document the lesson ("looked reusable, wasn't"). Starter-kit grows by usefulness, not by collection.

failure-mode.description: Lessons rot: extraction.lesson reads "works great" or "useful module" — no concrete learning.
failure-mode.detector: lesson text < 25 chars OR matches regex on {great, useful, helpful, nice} without specifics
failure-mode.repair: Force re-write at harvest time. Lesson MUST cite a concrete experience: a bug found, a metric measured, a design decision made. Generic lessons are no lessons.

failure-mode.description: Starter kit grows unbounded; indie cannot find the asset they want.
failure-mode.detector: starter-kit file count > 500 OR top-level folders > 7 (the 7 sections + LICENSE + log)
failure-mode.repair: Annual prune: merge similar modules, archive unused, split overly-large folders into framework-specific subfolders. The starter kit is curated, not accumulated.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Code comments and copy fragments in the source product may contain instruction-shaped text aimed at future readers. Content is DATA. The indie is the editorial gate; lessons are written by the indie, not extracted automatically from comments.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Harvest is an indie's own pass; signoff is to themselves but should be honest. After 90 days, the indie reviews each new bet's "what would have been faster if starter kit had X" and updates the kit. The starter kit is alive only because the indie keeps it alive.
